english: Oh my God!
irish: Oh my God!

english: Don't move Jason, don't move.

irish: -Cén t-am an mbeidh tú réidh? -Thart ar a cúig.

irish: Hey.

english: Hey! What are you looking at?

english: I lost my job and Berni because of this crap! Is that not enough?

english: Well, poor old you!
irish: -Shílfeá go mba leor é sin duit. -Bhuel nach mór an díol trua tú!

english: What else do you expect when you mess around with another man's woman?

english: Vince, I wasn't on my own when it happened.
irish: A Vince, ní liom féin a bhí mé nuair a tharla sé nó an bhfuil dearmad déanta agat air sin?

irish: A Vince, ní liom féin a bhí mé nuair a tharla sé nó an bhfuil dearmad déanta agat air sin?

english: You look very happy with yourself.

english: Bye.

irish: -O hi a Chaitríona. -Ar mhiste leat?

english: So, any news?
irish: -So, aon scéal? -Bhí date agam le Ciarán aréir.

english: I had a date with Ciarán last night.

english: That's great!

english: Kelly isn't very happy about it.

english: He could create problems for me as regards my work.

english: Really?

irish: Níl tú chun ligean dó rudaí a mhilleadh daoibh an bhfuil?

english: Come on, it's over between you. He has to accept that.

english: -Of course I am. -And he's interested in you?

english: Right, there's no problem then. Forget about Kelly.

english: Go for it before he's snatched up by some other woman.

irish: Ní thógfaidh sé i bhfad do stumpa breá mar sin.

irish: Come on a Jason! Céard atá air?

english: Jason are you alright?

english: You stay out of this. You've done enough damage as it is.
irish: Tá do dhóthain díobhála déanta agat mar atá tú. Gabh i leith uait a Jason

english: -We'd better take him to hospital. -What's wrong with him?

irish: Tabharfaidh mise isteach ag an ospidéal é, s'é mo mhac é!

english: Alright, but I'm going with you.
irish: -Alright, ach rachaidh mise leat. -Ceart go leor.

irish: -B'fhéidir gurbh fhearr duit do chara a thabhairt abhaile a Ríona. -Okay mar sin.

english: Yes.
irish: Sea.

english: God only knows how many men that woman has gone through!

english: It's true.

english: Do you know anyone else who has been married and separated twice?

english: Sarah, please.

irish: Frig Tadhg ar chuma ar bith.

english: Listen, you're exhausted. Come home and I'll cook you something.

english: I don't understand why he told him. What did he have to gain from it?
irish: Ní thuigim cén fáth gur inis sé dó é. Cén buntáiste a bhí ag baint leis?

english: It looks as bad for him as it does for me.
irish: Breathnaíonn seisean chomh dona céanna liomsa.

english: I can't make head nor tail of that man.
irish: A muise anois, níl ciall ná réasún leis an mboc sin.

english: I never thought he'd endanger his relationship with Jason in that way.
irish: Níor cheap mé go gcuirfeadh sé a chaidreamh le Jason ariamh i mbaol mar sin.

english: What do you mean?
Maybe someone else told him about it.
irish: B'fhéidir gur inis duine éigin eile dó faoi.

english: Jason's gone now and nothing can be done about it.
irish: Is tusa a rinne é.

english: You told him.
irish: Is tusa a rinne é.

irish: ..gur imigh sé anois seachas fanacht go dtí gur chuir tú do mhuinín as arís.

english: -I know it doesn't..! -Shut up!
irish: -Tá a fhios agam nach bhfeiceann tú mar sin anois é ach..! -Dún do bhéal!

english: I don't want to hear another word from you!
irish: Ní theastaíonn uaim focal amháin eile a chloisteáil uait!

english: You've ruined my life yet again! Do you realise that?
irish: Tá mo shaol millte agat arís! An dtuigeann tú é sin?

english: He was never good enough for you. I did it for your benefit.

english: For my benefit? For my benefit?
irish: Ar mhaithe liomsa? Ar mhaithe liomsa?

english: Is that why you hit me as well? For my own benefit?

english: Don't start that again. I didn't mean to do it..!
irish: A Suzy, ná tosaigh é sin arís. Tá a fhios agat go maith nach in aon turas a rinne mé..!

english: You're sick! Do you understand that?
irish: Tá tusa tinn. An dtuigeann tú é sin? Go huile agus go hiomlán as do bhleeding meabhair!

irish: Tá tusa tinn. An dtuigeann tú é sin? Go huile agus go hiomlán as do bhleeding meabhair!

english: Suzy!
irish: A Suzy!

english: Right, go on.
irish: Right, cinnte, abair leat.

english: I've been thinking about what we said last night.

english: Right.
irish: Right.

irish: Agus tá mo jab an-tábhachtach dom.

english: I'm not going to let people push me around anymore.

english: I'll live my life the way I see fit from now on.
irish: S'é mo chomhairle féin a bheidh mé ag tógáil as seo amach.

irish: An bhfuil gach rud ceart go leor?

english: Séamus, why won't you tell me what's the matter with you?
irish: Cén fáth nach n-insíonn tusa dom céard atá suas leat a Shéamuis?

english: Are you going to make the tea or do I have to do it myself?
irish: An bhfuil tú ag dul ag réiteach an tae sin nó an gcaithfidh mé féin é a réiteach?

english: Don't you realise that I'm worried about you?
irish: Ach nach bhfuil a fhios agat go bhfuil imní orm fút?

english: If so, you're wasting your time.
irish: Má tá, tá tú ag cur do chuid ama amú.

english: How can I help you when you won't tell me what's wrong with you?
irish: Agus cén chaoi is féidir liom cabhrú leat nuair nach n-insíonn tú dom go díreach céard atá ort?

english: I don't want your help.
irish: Níl mise ag iarraidh do chuid cúnaimh.

english: Telling you wouldn't help matters.
irish: Agus ar aon nós, ní dhéanfaidh sé aon mhaith duit é a inseacht duit.

english: Try me!
irish: Féach leis é. As ucht Mac dílis Dé ort agus inis dom cén deamhan...

english: For the love of God, tell me what the hell is bothering you, Séamus.
irish: ..nó an diabhal atá ag cur as duit a Shéamuis.

english: I'm losing my sight.
irish: Tá mé ag cailleadh amharc na súil.

english: I'm going blind.
irish: Tá mé ag fáil dall.

english: That's what's wrong.
irish: Sin é atá orm.

english: You're a great comfort to me.
irish: Is é an rud amháin, a bhain beagán sóláis díot.

english: I just wish I had told you sooner. Frig it!
irish: Cén fáth nach fada ón lá a d'inis mé duit? Ara frig é!

english: How do you know?
irish: Cén chaoi a bhfuil a fhios agat é sin?

english: What do you mean, how do I know?
irish: Cén sórt, cén chaoi a bhfuil a fhios agam é?

english: How do you know that you're going blind?
irish: -Cén chaoi a bhfuil a fhios agat go bhfuil tú ag éirí dall? -Cén sórt friggin ceiste í sin?

english: What kind of a question is that?
irish: -Cén chaoi a bhfuil a fhios agat go bhfuil tú ag éirí dall? -Cén sórt friggin ceiste í sin?

english: It's a very reasonable question, you know.

english: I'm losing sight in both my eyes!
irish: Tá an t-amharc ag imeacht as mo dhá shúil.

english: When I strain them trying to read those bloody forms...
irish: Nuair a thagann an brú orthu ag léamh bitchseannaí forms... ..an chéad rud eile faighim tinneas cinn sa chloigeann.

english: ..I get headaches.
irish: ..an chéad rud eile faighim tinneas cinn sa chloigeann.

english: Then I get all flustered.
irish: Ansin tá mo chloigeann gaibhte trína chéile.

english: Lord above, we have to ring the doctor.
irish: O a Dhia dílis dá réiteach, ach caithfimid fios a chur ar an dochtúir, bíodh a fhios agat.

english: Blast you and your doctors.

english: No doctor can help me! Once you start losing your sight...
irish: Níl aon dochtúir in ann aon mhaith a dhéanamh dom.

english: ..there's nothing you can do about it and that's that.
irish: Nuair atá do chuid súl ag imeacht, tá siad imithe agus sin é a bhfuil faoi.

english: Good God!
irish: O a Thiarna!

irish: -A Ríona. -A Daniel.

english: I'm looking for Suzy. Is she here?

english: -Do you know where she is? -I have no idea.

irish: -Bhí sí trína chéile ar ball. -An raibh? Agus cén fáth?

english: You know, Jason and everything.
irish: Tá a fhios agat féin. Jason agus chuile shórt.

irish: -Right. -Tá mé an-imníoch fúithi a Ríona.

english: Well if I see her, I'll be sure to tell her. Okay?
irish: Right, bhuel má bhím ag caint léi, inseoidh mé sin di, okay.

english: Right.

english: Goodbye.
irish: Slán.

english: Well, he's gone.

irish: -Céard a dúirt sé? -Just go raibh imní air fút.

english: Are you sure you want to do this Suzy?
irish: An bhfuil tú cinnte go dteastaíonn uait é seo a dhéanamh a Suzy?

english: Yes.
irish: Tá.

english: -Hiya. -Hi Mack.
irish: -Hi. -O hi a Mhack.

english: So, any news?
irish: -So, aon scéal? -Bhuel tá mar a tharlaíonn sé.

english: Yes, actually. Suzy has given a statement against her father.

-You're happy about it, right? -Yes, but..!
irish: -Tá tú sásta faoi nach bhfuil? -Bhuel tá cinnte ach..!

english: But what?
irish: -Ach céard? -Bhuel má leanaimid leis seo...

english: If we go ahead with this, chances are that Daniel will file charges...
irish: ..tá seans ann go rachaidh Daniel i do dhiaidh tar éis an ionsaí a dhein tú air le tamall anuas.

english: ..against you for your assault on him.
irish: Breathnaigh a Oonagh, is cuma liomsa céard a dhéanfaidh sé.

irish: Breathnaigh a Oonagh, is cuma liomsa céard a dhéanfaidh sé.

english: If I have to do time in prison so that he'll get his comeuppance...
irish: -I ndáiríre? -Dá mbheadh orm téarma príosúin a dhéanamh,... ..má chiallaíonn sé go bhfaighfear greim air sin, déanfaidh mé é.

english: ..I'll do it.
irish: -An bhfuil tú cinnte faoi sin a Mhack -Chomh siúráilte agus atá an cac san asal.

english: -Are you sure about that? -Positive.
irish: Breathnaigh, fair play duit a sis. Feicfidh mé ar ball tú.

english: Fair play sis. I'll see you later.
irish: Breathnaigh, fair play duit a sis. Feicfidh mé ar ball tú.

english: In a way, I believe him.
irish: Ar bhealach éicint creidim é.

english: Am I stupid to think that after what he's done to me?
irish: An bhfuil mé dúr a bheith ag cuimhneamh mar sin tar éis an méid atá déanta aige orm?

english: He told you about it himself?
irish: Breathnaigh, sé féin a d'inis duit i dtosach é, nach é?

irish: Sé.

english: And did you believe what he had to say?
irish: Agus ar chreid tú a raibh le rá aige?

english: Well, that's the thing.
irish: Bhuel, sin é an rud.

english: I don't know.
irish: Níl a fhios agam.

english: Máire, how did you and Peadar manage to stay together all these years?
irish: A Mháire, cén chaoi ar éirigh leat féin agus Peadar fanacht le chéile thar na blianta?

english: Peadar and I had our fair share of problems.
irish: Bhí ár gcuid fadhbanna féin agamsa agus ag Peadar, a Bherni.

english: And they weren't your average ups and downs either.
irish: Agus ní haon thíos seal, thuas seal, céanna atá i gceist agatsa a bhí ann i gcónaí ach an oiread.

english: They were much more serious than that.
irish: Ach rudaí a bhí i bhfad níos tromchúisí.

english: Máire, Peadar didn't do something like that to you, did he?
irish: A Mháire, ní dhearna Peadar rud mar sin ortsa riamh, an ndearna?

english: Peadar didn't do anything.
irish: Ní dhearna Peadar tada, a Bherni.

english: You have to make up your own mind.
irish: Caithfidh tusa do chinneadh féin a dhéanamh anois a Bherni.

english: You have to think of yourself and Evan.
irish: Ort féin agus ar Evainín a chaithfeas tusa cuimhneamh.

english: And above all else, you have to ask yourself whether you have enough...
irish: Agus thar rud ar bith eile, caithfidh tú fiafraí díot féin... ..an bhfuil do dhóthain grá agat do Rory i do chroí istigh...

english: ..love in your heart for Rory to be able to forgive him for what he did.
irish: ..le go mbeidh tú in ann an rud a rinne sé ort a mhaithiú dó.

english: I knew it!
irish: -Bhí a fhios agam é! -Bhí a fhios agat! Bhí a fhios agat!

english: Indeed!
irish: -Bhí a fhios agam é! -Bhí a fhios agat! Bhí a fhios agat!

english: I told you that you weren't going blind but you wouldn't believe me.
irish: Dúirt mé leat nach raibh tú ag éirí caoch ach ní chreidfeá mé.

english: Where are they? Take them out and put them on.
irish: Ach cá bhfuil siad anois? Tóg amach iad agus cuir ort iad.

english: Or before long you'll be blind for certain!
irish: Nó is gearr go mbeidh tú caoch i ndáiríre, bíodh a fhios agat.

english: Was I not right all along?
irish: Agus anois an raibh an ceart agam?

english: After telling me that I had lost the plot!
irish: Agus tú ag rá liomsa go raibh mé imithe sna cearca fraoigh.

english: You have me spoiled, you know.
irish: An bhfuil a fhios agat céard é féin ach tá mé millte agat.

english: I know darling.
irish: Tá a fhios agam a stór.

english: So, how was work today?
irish: So, cén chaoi a raibh an obair inniu?

english: Crazy.
irish: Craiceáilte. Caithfidh go n-airíonn sibh Rory uaibh?

english: You must miss Rory.
irish: Caithfidh go n-airíonn sibh Rory uaibh?

english: Because of the work load, I mean.
irish: Mar gheall ar an méid oibre, atá i gceist agam.

english: We're busy but we're coping without him.
irish: Bhuel tá muid gnóthach ach tá muid ag cópáil dá oireasa.

english: Look, I'm sorry darling.
irish: Breathnaigh tá brón orm, a stór.

english: I didn't mean to make you feel uncomfortable.
irish: Ní raibh mé ag iarraidh tú a dhéanamh míchompordach.

english: It's alright.
irish: Tá sé ceart go leor.

english: Mack attacked Daniel a while back.
irish: D'ionsaigh Mack Daniel, tamaillín ó shin.

english: That's the reason why Mack had to leave for a while.
irish: Agus is mar gheall air sin a raibh ar Mack imeacht ar feadh tamaillín.

english: Daniel has been threatening to file charges since then.
irish: Bhuel, tá Daniel ag bagairt an dlí air ó shin agus má chuireann..!

english: And if he does..!
irish: Ní bhreathnódh sé ró-mhaith duit.

english: It won't look very good for you.
irish: Ní bhreathnódh sé ró-mhaith duit.

english: No.
irish: No.

english: And if Kelly says anything about us on top of that..!
irish: Agus má dheireann Kelly aon rud fúinn ar a mhullach sin..!

english: I see.
irish: Tuigim.

english: That's not what I mean.
irish: O ní sin atá i gceist agam.

english: Are you sure about that?
irish: An bhfuil tú lán-chinnte faoi sin?

english: Look, I understand your situation Oonagh.
irish: Look, tuigim do chás go huile agus go hiomlán a Oonagh.

english: It's your choice, okay?
irish: Agus is do rogha féin é, okay?

english: Yeah, I know.
irish: Yeah, tá a fhios agam.

english: Thanks love, dinner was delicious.
irish: Go raibh míle maith agat a stór. Bhí an dinnéar go hálainn.

english: We aim to please!
irish: Oh, we aim to please! Agus ar aon chaoi, caithfidh mé a chruthú gur fiú duit mé a bheith thart.

english: Besides, I have to prove that I'm worth having around.
irish: Oh, we aim to please! Agus ar aon chaoi, caithfidh mé a chruthú gur fiú duit mé a bheith thart.

english: What's the matter?
irish: Céard atá ort?

english: Come on Caitríona. I was only joking.
irish: Come on a Chaitríona! Ní raibh mé ach ag magadh.

english: This is not going to work Vince.
irish: Ní oibreoidh sé seo a Vince.

english: What have I done?
irish: Céard a rinne mé?

english: Even with Rory gone from the radio station...
irish: Fiú agus Rory imithe ón stáisiún raidió, tá éad ort.

english: ..you're jealous.
Jesus, of course I'm jealous!
irish: Jesus, cinnte tá éad orm! Chodail tú leis in ainm Dé!

english: You slept with him, for crying out loud!
Can you hear the anger in your voice?
irish: An gcloiseann tú an fearg i do ghlór?

english: You can't let it go.
irish: Níl tú in ann ligint leis.

english: Give me a chance.
irish: Tabhair seans dom. Tá mé ag oibriú air.

english: I'm working on it.
That's the problem.
irish: Sin í an fhadhb.

english: That I'm trying to put it behind me?
irish: Go bhfuil mé ag déanamh iarracht é a chur taobh thiar díom?

english: Isn't that what you wanted?
irish: Nach shin é a bhí tú ag iarraidh? Sé.

english: Yes.
irish: Sé.

english: But you're going overboard.
irish: Ach tá tú ag dul thar fóir. An fíon, an dinnéar.

english: Wine, dinner.
irish: An fíon, an dinnéar.

english: Waiting for me to get back from work as if I were a schoolgirl.
irish: Ag fanacht liom teacht ón obair ar nós gur cailín óg scoile mé.

english: I'm doing my best, Caitríona.
irish: Tá mé ag déanamh mo dhícheall a Chaitríona.

english: I know.
irish: Tá a fhios agam.

english: So what's the problem?
irish: Bhuel céard í an fhadhb mar sin?

english: You can't do it, Vince.
irish: Níl tú in ann a Vince.

english: You can't forgive me and you might never be able to.
irish: Níl tú in ann é a mhaithiú dom agus b'fhéidir nach mbeidh go deo.

english: Thanks very much for the vote of confidence.
irish: O bhuel, go raibh míle maith agat as ucht an vóta muiníne.

english: I'm sorry.
irish: Tá brón orm.

english: Caitríona, we can get through this.
irish: A Chaitríona, is féidir linn teacht trí seo.

english: I'm willing to give it a shot.
irish: Tá mise sásta iarracht a dhéanamh.

english: Aren't you?
irish: Nach bhfuil tusa?

english: There's nothing I'd like more.
irish: Níl rud ar bith ab fhearr liom.

english: But I just don't know Vince.
irish: Ach níl a fhios agam a Vince.

english: If you can't let it go, is it worth the effort?
irish: Muna bhfuil tú in ann scaoileadh leis..! Ar fiú é?

english: Another one.
irish: -Ceann eile ansin. -Meas tú an mbeidh do chuid duán in ann aige?

english: Do you think your kidneys are up to it?
irish: -Ceann eile ansin. -Meas tú an mbeidh do chuid duán in ann aige?

english: Aisling, another one please.
irish: Aisling, ceann eile más é do thoil é.

english: -The craic is about to begin! -For God's sake Mam, stop!
irish: Beidh craic anois ann. In ainm Dé a Mhama an stopfaidh tú!

english: Look, I hate what I've done to you.
irish: Breathnaigh, is fuath liom an rud a rinne mé ort.

english: I can't believe that I've hurt you in this way.
irish: Ní chreidim gur ghortaigh mé mar sin tú.

english: I know that nothing I say will change what happened.
irish: Ach tá a fhios agam nach n-athróidh tada a deirim leat, an rud a tharla.

english: No Rory, it won't.
irish: No a Rory, ní athróidh.

english: All the same, I think that we should give it another try.
irish: Ach, fós féin, ceapaim gur chóir dúinn triail eile a bhaint as.

english: Really?
irish: -I ndáiríre? -I ndáiríre.

english: Really.
irish: -I ndáiríre? -I ndáiríre.

english: Six months in Slovenia! I'd be mad not to do it.
irish: Sé mhí i Slovenia! Sure bheinn craiceáilte gan é a dhéanamh.

english: What about your job here?
irish: Yeah ach céard faoi do jab anseo?

english: I'm going to ask Labhrás if I can take a career break.
irish: Bhuel, tá sé i gceist agam fiafraí de Labhrás an bhféadfainn sos gairme a thógáil.

english: A career break? After all the time you've taken off already!
irish: Sos gairme? Tar éis an méid am saoire a thóg tú cheana féin i mbliana? A Róise, níl hope agat!

english: You don't have a hope, Róise.
irish: -Ach má dheireann mé le Caitríona é a rá ar mo shon. -Caitríona? Bhuel good luck.

english: I'm going to ask Caitríona to ask him on my behalf.
irish: -Ach má dheireann mé le Caitríona é a rá ar mo shon. -Caitríona? Bhuel good luck.

english: Caitríona? Well good luck!
Two bottles of lager please.
irish: -Dhá bhuidéal lager más é do thoil é. -Actually, ní bheidh ceann ar bith agamsa.

english: Actually not for me.
irish: -Cén fáth? -Caithfidh mé imeacht?

english: -Why not? -I have to go.
irish: -Cén fáth? -Caithfidh mé imeacht?

english: What's up?
irish: Ach céard atá ort?

english: Suzy is a bit upset. I have to meet her.
irish: -Tá Suzy cineáilín upset. Caithfidh mé dul agus í a mheeteáil. -O iontach.

english: Oh great.
irish: -Breathnaigh, beidh mé ag caint amárach leat okay. -Yeah, amárach.

english: -I'll talk to you tomorrow. -Yeah, tomorrow.
irish: -Breathnaigh, beidh mé ag caint amárach leat okay. -Yeah, amárach.

english: Mr. McLoughlin, I'd be grateful if you would accompany me...
irish: A Uasail Mhic Loughlin, bheinn buíoch dá dtiocfá liom go dtí an stáisiún le cúpla ceist a fhreagairt.

english: ..to the station to answer a few questions.
Now, if you don't mind.
irish: -Anois, muna mhiste leat. -Imigh leat maith an bhean.

english: Clear off good woman.
irish: Mar a dúirt mé, bheinn buíoch díot teacht..!

english: As I said, I'd be grateful if..!
irish: Mar a dúirt mé, bheinn buíoch díot teacht..!

english: I heard you and I gave you my answer. Now leave me be or you'll be sorry.
irish: Chuala me tú agus thug mé freagra ort! Anois éist liom, nó beidh aiféala ort.

english: I will not repeat myself.
irish: Ní dhéarfaidh mé uair amháin eile é.

english: When do you intend stopping this nonsense?
irish: Cén uair atá sé i gceist agat éirí as an tseafóid seo?

english: There are laws to protect people from this kind of thing.
irish: Tá dlíthe ann le daoine a chosaint ar a leithéid, bíodh a fhios agat.

english: With you harassing me and your brother attacking me...
irish: Idir tusa do mo chéasadh agus do dheartháir do mo ionsaí...

english: ..what exactly has the O Riain family got against me?
irish: ..cén fhadhb go díreach atá ag clann Uí Riain liom?

english: Do you want me to move out?
irish: An bhfuil tú ag iarraidh orm bogadh amach mar sin?

english: Right.
irish: Right.

english: Caitríona, I don't want to lose what we have.
irish: A Chaitríona, ní theastaíonn uaim an rud atá againn a chailleadh.

english: Neither do I.
irish: Ní theastaíonn ná uaimse.

english: But I'm afraid that it has already been lost.
irish: Ach tá faitíos orm go bhfuil sé caillte cheana féin.

english: Vince, please!
irish: A Vince, le do thoil.

english: This is hard enough.
irish: Tá sé seo sách deacair.

english: Right.
irish: Right.

english: I'm sorry.
irish: Tá brón orm.

english: Me too.
irish: Agus ormsa.

english: -Hi! -Hi.
irish: -Hi. -Hi.

english: This is a nice surprise.
irish: Is deas an surprise é seo.

english: I didn't expect to see you again today.
irish: Ní raibh súil ar bith agam go bhfeicfinn arís inniu tú.

english: What's the matter?
irish: Céard atá mícheart?

english: I could have done without the dramatics today, to be honest.
irish: D'fhéadfainn a dhéanamh gan na dramatics a bhí againn inniu a Ríona le bheith fírinneach leat.

english: I know, I'm sorry about that.
irish: Yeah, tá a fhios agam. Tá brón orm faoi sin.

english: It's not your fault.
irish: Níl ortsa atá an locht. Caithfimid a bheith cúramach anseo, tuigeann tú é sin nach dtuigeann?

english: We have to be careful, you understand that don't you?
irish: Yes, tuigim.

english: Yes, I do.
irish: Yes, tuigim.

english: I think they thought you were just giving me a lift home.
irish: Look, ar aon nós, ceapaim gur cheap siad nach raibh tú ach just ag tabhairt síob abhaile dom.

english: Which was true.
irish: Rud a bhí fíor. O come on, ná bí mar seo.

english: Come on, don't be like that!
irish: Tá alán le cailleadh agam anseo. Má fhaigheann Lillian amach..!

english: I have a lot to lose. If Lillian finds out..!
irish: Tá alán le cailleadh agam anseo. Má fhaigheann Lillian amach..!

english: I don't want to be in a situation where I won't see my family again.
irish: Nílim ag iarraidh a bheith sa riocht agus nach bhfeicfidh mé mo chlann arís.

english: You understand that, don't you?
irish: -Tuigeann tú é sin nach dtuigeann? -Yes tuigim!

english: Yes! You remind me often enough.
irish: Cuireann tú i gcuimhne dom sách minic é.

english: Ríona!
irish: A Ríona!

english: I'm sorry.
irish: Tá brón orm. Táim braon den chur i gcéill seo, sin an méid.

english: I'm just sick of this pretence.
irish: Yeah bhuel tá beirt againn ann.

english: You're not the only one.
irish: Tá a fhios agam. Inseoidh mé di go luath.

english: I know. I'll tell her soon. It's not the right time yet.
irish: Níl an t-am ceart fós. Ach is leatsa atáim ag iarraidh a bheith. Tá a fhios agat faoi sin nach bhfuil?

english: It's you I want to be with. You know that, don't you?
irish: Níl an t-am ceart fós. Ach is leatsa atáim ag iarraidh a bheith. Tá a fhios agat faoi sin nach bhfuil?

english: What do you want?
irish: -Céard atá uait? -Shínigh mé ráiteas in aghaidh m'athar.

english: I signed a statement against my father.
-And? -You said you'd stand by me.
irish: -Agus? -Dúirt tú go seasfá liom.

english: That's before I found out that you sold my son.
irish: Sin sula raibh a fhios agam gur dhíol tú mo mhac. Ach ní raibh sé i gceist agam riamh dul ar aghaidh leis.

english: I never meant to go ahead with it.
irish: Ach ní raibh sé i gceist agam riamh dul ar aghaidh leis.

english: Why would I believe anything you say?
irish: Cén fáth a gcreidfinn focal as do bhéal? Cuir ceist ar Thadhg. Dúirt mé leis é agus le hAngela.

english: Ask Tadhg. I told him and Angela.
irish: Cuir ceist ar Thadhg. Dúirt mé leis é agus le hAngela.

english: That's easy to say.
irish: Is furasta rudaí a rá.

english: How did you think up such a thing in the first place?
irish: Cén chaoi ar smaoinigh tú ar rud mar sin sa gcéad áit? Ní mé a smaoinigh air. Tháinig Tadhg chugamsa agus bhagair sé orm.

english: I didn't think of it. Tadhg came to me and threatened me.
irish: O yeah!

english: -Oh yeah. -That's the truth.
irish: Sí an fhírinne í a Jason.

english: You're being very high and mighty...
irish: Ach nach tusa an ceann atá mórálach...

english: ..about a baby you had no interest in until you found out he was dying.
irish: ..faoi leanbh nach raibh suim dá laghad agat ann go dtí go bhfuair tú amach go raibh sé ag fáil bháis?

english: At least I didn't try to friggin' sell him!
irish: Ar a laghad níor thriail mé é a friggin dhíol!

english: Believe what you will.
irish: Creid cibé rud a theastaíonn uait.

english: But I'm telling the truth.
irish: Ach tá mé ag inseacht na fírinne.

english: I did what you asked of me.
irish: Anois, rinne mé an rud a d'iarr tú orm.

english: I chose you over my father.
irish: Roghnaigh mé tusa seachas mo Dhaid.

english: I hope it's not too late for you to stand by me, like you promised.
irish: Agus tá súil agam nach bhfuil sé ró-mhall duit seasamh liom. Mar a gheall tú.

english: You're still here I see, Daniel?
irish: Shílfeá go bhfuil tú fós anseo a Daniel.

english: Why wouldn't I be?
irish: Is tuige nach mbeinn? O níl a fhios agam. Pionta a Thaidhg.

english: I don't know. A pint, Tadhg.
irish: O níl a fhios agam. Pionta a Thaidhg.

english: Maybe the game's not over after all.
irish: O, b'fhéidir nach bhfuil an cluiche thart fós.

english: Daniel McLoughlin, I'm arresting you under Section 3..!
irish: -Daniel McLoughlin, tá mé do do ghabháil faoi Alt 3..! -Céard sa diabhal atá ar siúl agat?

english: What the hell are you doing?
irish: Den Bhille Um Chiontaithe Neamhmharfacha 1997 in Aghaidh an Duine.

english: Of the Non-Fatal Offences Against the Person Act, 1997.
irish: Cén sórt ráiméise cainte é sin a bhean?

english: What kind of nonsense is this, woman?
irish: Mar gheall ar an ionsaí ar dhein tú ar d'iníon, Suzy McLoughlin, ar an 15ú Aibreán, 2004.

english: For the attack you carried out on Suzy McLoughlin on April 15th 2004.
irish: Ná bí seafóideach, ní dhearna mise é sin!

english: -Don't be stupid, I didn't do that! -You have a right to remain silent.
irish: Ní gá duit aon ní a rá muna mian leat féin é ach aon rud a deireann tú,...

english: Whatever you say may be taken down and used against you.
irish: -..tógfar síos i scríbhinn é i d'aghaidhse. -Ní féidir leat é seo a dhéanamh.

english: -You can't do this! -Go ahead.
irish: -Lean ort. -Eirigh as! Eirigh as! Eirigh as a dúirt mé!

english: Stop it! Stop it I said!
irish: -Lean ort. -Eirigh as! Eirigh as! Eirigh as a dúirt mé!

english: Go ahead and enjoy the show but you won't get away with it!
irish: Bíodh do seó mór agat mar sin ach ní thabharfaidh tú na cosa leat.

english: Do you really think that you can convict me of something I didn't do?
irish: An gceapann tú i ndáiríre gur féidir leat mé a chiontú as rud nach ndearna mé ar chor ar bith!

english: -And for which you have no evidence! -I have evidence.
irish: -Agus nach bhfuil fianaise ar bith agatsa i mo leith! -Tá fianaise agam.

english: What kind of bloody evidence?
irish: -Cén sórt bloody fianaise! -Tá ráiteas agam ó d'iníon...

english: Your daughter made a statement saying that you attacked her that night.
irish: ..a dheimhníonn gur tusa a d'ionsaigh í an oíche sin.

english: Jesus!
irish: Jesus!

english: It's all lies and well you know it!
irish: Is bréaga ar fad atá anseo agus is maith atá a fhios agaibh é!

english: Take him out, lads.
irish: Tóg amach é a leaids.

english: Suzy! How could you do this to me?
irish: A Suzy! Cén chaoi a bhféadfá é seo a dhéanamh orm?

english: Suzy!
irish: A Suzy!

english: What did I do to you to deserve this?
irish: Céard a rinne mé ort le go mbeadh sé seo tuillte agam?
Fotheidil: Europus
